## COMPREHENSIVE TLDRAW INTEGRATION ANALYSIS

### 1. PACKAGE STRUCTURE: `@haklex/rich-ext-tldraw`

**Location:** `<REPO_ROOT>/haklex/rich-ext-tldraw/`

**Package.json Details:**
- Version: `0.0.50`
- Main dependency: `tldraw: ^4.4.0`
- Type: ESM only
- Exports:
  - `.` (main edit entry)
  - `./static` (read-only static entry, for SSR/display)
  - `./style.css` (styles)

### 2. CORE FILES & ARCHITECTURE

#### **2.1 Node Layer (DecoratorNode)**

**`TldrawNode.ts`** - Base read-only node (for static rendering)
```
- extends DecoratorNode<ReactElement>
- stores: __snapshot (string) - serialized tldraw state
- methods:
  - getSnapshot() / setSnapshot()
  - importJSON(serializedNode.snapshot)
  - exportJSON() → { type: 'tldraw', snapshot, version: 1 }
  - decorate() → TldrawSSRRenderer (server-side placeholder)
- getType() returns 'tldraw'
```

**`TldrawEditNode.ts`** - Edit-enabled node extension
```
- extends TldrawNode
- slashMenuItems: registers "Whiteboard" option in slash menu
- decorate() returns:
  - if editable: LazyEditRenderer wrapped in Suspense
  - if readonly: LazyDisplayRenderer wrapped in Suspense
- lazy loading via React.lazy() for both renderers
```

#### **2.2 Renderer Layer**

**`TldrawSSRRenderer.tsx`** - Server-side rendering placeholder
```
- minimal render: 16/10 aspect ratio box
- parses snapshot to count shapes: `shape:` prefixed keys
- shows: "Tldraw Whiteboard" or "Tldraw Whiteboard (N shapes)"
```

**`TldrawDisplayRenderer.tsx`** - Read-only display component
```
- lazy-loaded in production renderer
- uses IntersectionObserver for lazy-loading (200px margin)
- features:
  - zoom in/out buttons
  - "fit to content" button
  - fullscreen expand button (via presentDialog modal)
- tldraw loaded via dynamic import('tldraw')
- readonly mode: editor.updateInstanceState({ isReadonly: true })
- theme sync: follows colorScheme context
```

**`TldrawEditRenderer.tsx`** - Interactive edit component
```
- lazy-loaded in editor mode
- shows preview + "Edit Whiteboard" overlay button
- fullscreen dialog for editing:
  - header with title, meta, status dot
  - storage mode toggle: inline vs remote
  - manual save button (if remote storage configured)
  - export JSON / copy JSON buttons
  - copy remote ref button (if saved remotely)
  - unsaved changes confirmation (ESC key interception)
- auto-save: debounced 2000ms
- two storage modes:
  a) inline: snapshot stored as JSON string in node
  b) remote: snapshot stored in external service, node stores ref URL
```

#### **2.3 Plugin & Command**

**`TldrawPlugin.tsx`**
```
- registers INSERT_TLDRAW_COMMAND
- creates TldrawEditNode with empty snapshot '{}'
- inserted via slash menu or direct command dispatch
```

#### **2.4 Configuration Context**

**`TldrawConfigContext.tsx`**
```
interface TldrawConfig {
  apiUrl?: string
  saveSnapshot?: (snapshot: object) => Promise<string>
}
- Provider wraps editor/renderer
- useTldrawConfig() hook to access config
```

#### **2.5 Data Loading Hook**

**`useTldrawData.ts`** - Smart snapshot resolution
```
Three modes:
1. Inline JSON: `snapshot: { shapes: {...}, pages: {...}, ... }`
   → parsed directly, rendered immediately

2. Remote URL reference: `ref:abc123`
   → fetched from `{apiUrl}/objects/{refId}`
   → only if apiUrl configured

3. Remote direct URL: `https://...`
   → fetched directly as-is

Error handling:
- Missing apiUrl for ref: mode → error state
- Invalid JSON → tries remote fetch
- Fetch failure → error state
```

#### **2.6 Styling**

**`styles.css.ts`** - Vanilla Extract CSS-in-TS
```
Components:
- tldrawStaticContainer / tldrawEditorContainer (16:10 aspect ratio)
- tldrawLoading (spinner animation)
- tldrawError (caution bg color)
- tldrawActionGroup (bottom-right zoom/fit buttons)
- tldrawActionButton (zoom controls with hover states)
- tldrawFullscreenPopup (overrides editor-ui dialog to fullscreen)
- tldrawDialogHeader (with status dot, title, meta, actions)
- tldrawHeaderActions (storage mode toggle, save btn, export btns)
- tldrawActionBarBtn / tldrawActionBarSep (header action bar items)
- tldrawDialogCanvas (flex:1, editor area)
- tldrawConfirmActions / tldrawConfirmBtn (unsaved changes dialog)
- tldrawEditOverlay (hover-to-show edit button)
```

#### **2.7 Markdown Transformer**

**`transformer.ts`** (re-exported from @haklex/rich-headless)
```
export TLDRAW_BLOCK_TRANSFORMER:
- export: node.type === 'tldraw'
  → `<tldraw>\n${snapshot}\n</tldraw>`
- used in Lexical → Markdown conversion
- no import pattern (display-only)
```

### 3. HEADLESS NODE (SERVER-SIDE)

**`@haklex/rich-headless/src/index.ts`**
```
export const TldrawNode = headlessDecorator('tldraw', ['snapshot'], {
  snapshot: ''
})
- no React, no DOM
- used for JSON parsing only
- included in allHeadlessNodes
```

### 4. REGISTRATION & WIRING

#### **Static (Display) Pipeline**

**`@haklex/rich-renderers/src/tldraw.ts`**
```
exports from @haklex/rich-ext-tldraw/static:
- TldrawNode (base node)
- TldrawStaticRenderer (display component)
- $createTldrawNode / $isTldrawNode
- TldrawConfigProvider
```

**`@haklex/rich-renderers/package.json`**
```
exports:
- ./tldraw: ./src/tldraw.ts
includes: @haklex/rich-ext-tldraw as dependency
```

**`ShiroRenderer.tsx` integration**
```
import { TldrawNode } from '@haklex/rich-renderers'
const defaultExtraNodes = [TldrawNode, ...embedNodes, ...]
<RichRenderer extraNodes={mergedNodes} rendererConfig={enhancedRendererConfig} />
```

#### **Edit (Interactive) Pipeline**

**`@haklex/rich-renderers-edit/src/tldraw.ts`**
```
exports from @haklex/rich-ext-tldraw:
- TldrawEditNode (edit-enabled node)
- TldrawEditRenderer (interactive component)
- TldrawPlugin (command handler)
- INSERT_TLDRAW_COMMAND
```

**`@haklex/rich-renderers-edit/src/config.ts`**
```
enhancedEditRendererConfig (RendererConfig):
- spreads static config
- does NOT override a "Tldraw" renderer entry
  (display/edit handled via TldrawEditNode.decorate() logic)
```

**`ShiroEditor.tsx` integration**
```
import { TldrawEditNode, TldrawPlugin } from '@haklex/rich-renderers-edit'
const defaultExtraNodes = [TldrawEditNode, ...embedEditNodes, ...]
<RichEditor extraNodes={mergedNodes} rendererConfig={enhancedEditRendererConfig}>
  <TldrawPlugin />
  ...
</RichEditor>
```

#### **Kit Integration**

**`@haklex/rich-kit-shiro/src/index.ts`**
```
re-exports all from @haklex/rich-ext-tldraw:
- TldrawNode, TldrawEditNode
- TldrawRenderer, TldrawEditRenderer, TldrawDisplayRenderer
- TldrawPlugin, INSERT_TLDRAW_COMMAND
- TldrawConfigProvider, useTldrawConfig
- SerializedTldrawNode, TldrawConfig
```

**`@haklex/rich-kit-shiro/src/markdown.ts`**
```
imports TLDRAW_BLOCK_TRANSFORMER from @haklex/rich-ext-tldraw
SHIRO_EXT_MARKDOWN_TRANSFORMERS includes it
buildShiroMarkdownTransformers() includes all
```

### 5. DATA FORMAT & SERIALIZATION

#### **Serialized Node**
```typescript
type SerializedTldrawNode = {
  type: 'tldraw'
  snapshot: string
  version: 1
  ...SerializedLexicalNode
}
```

#### **Snapshot String Content**
Two possible formats:
1. **Inline JSON**: `"{\"records\":{\"shape:abc\":{...},\"page:xyz\":{...}},\"schema\":{...}}"`
2. **Remote Reference**: `"ref:object-id-hash"` (implies remote storage)

#### **Tldraw Store Format**
```
editor.store.getSnapshot() returns:
{
  "records": {
    "shape:uuid1": { id, type, x, y, props, ... },
    "shape:uuid2": { ... },
    ...
    "page:uuid": { id, name, gridSize, ... },
    ...
  },
  "schema": { version, ... }
}
```

### 6. LAZY LOADING STRATEGY

**Dynamic Import Points:**
```typescript
// TldrawEditNode.decorate()
const LazyDisplayRenderer = lazy(() =>
  import('./TldrawDisplayRenderer').then((m) => ({
    default: m.TldrawDisplayRenderer,
  }))
)
const LazyEditRenderer = lazy(() =>
  import('./TldrawEditRenderer').then((m) => ({
    default: m.TldrawEditRenderer,
  }))
)

// Both renderers
useEffect(() => {
  import('tldraw').then((mod) => {
    setTldrawComponent(() => mod.Tldraw)
    setLibLoading(false)
  })
}, [])
```

**Bundle Impact:**
- `tldraw` lib (4.4.0): ~1.5MB gzipped, lazy-loaded on demand
- Editor entry: includes TldrawEditNode + lazy renderers
- Renderer entry: includes TldrawNode + lazy display renderer
- Slash menu: triggers creation, which lazy-loads on first edit

**Fallback UI:**
- While loading: `"Loading tldraw..."` spinner
- If error: error message displayed
- Hydration warning suppressed via `suppressHydrationWarning`

### 7. INTEGRATION POINTS IN WEB APP

**No tldraw-specific code in `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src`**

Integration via:
1. Import `ShiroEditor` from `@haklex/rich-kit-shiro/editor`
2. `ShiroEditor` automatically includes `TldrawEditNode` + `TldrawPlugin`
3. Import `ShiroRenderer` from `@haklex/rich-kit-shiro/renderer`
4. `ShiroRenderer` automatically includes `TldrawNode`

Optional config:
```typescript
<TldrawConfigProvider apiUrl="..." saveSnapshot={async (doc) => ...}>
  <ShiroEditor />
</TldrawConfigProvider>
```

### 8. SLASH MENU INTEGRATION

**`TldrawEditNode.slashMenuItems`**
```
{
  title: 'Whiteboard',
  icon: PenTool (lucide-react),
  description: 'Tldraw whiteboard canvas',
  keywords: ['tldraw', 'whiteboard', 'draw', 'canvas'],
  section: 'MEDIA',
  onSelect: (editor) => {
    editor.update(() => {
      $insertNodes([$createTldrawEditNode('{}')])
    })
  }
}
```
- Appears in slash menu under MEDIA section
- Inserting creates new TldrawEditNode with empty snapshot

## Official Package: @excalidraw/excalidraw

The official Excalidraw package (@excalidraw/excalidraw) is version 0.18.0 with a package size of 46.8 MB as of the most recent update (3 days ago from the search results).

## What is Excalidraw?

Excalidraw is exported as a component to be directly embedded in your project, making it straightforward to integrate into React applications. You can install the package using npm or yarn with the command `npm install react react-dom @excalidraw/excalidraw`.

## Bundle Considerations

It's worth noting that the UMD build ships with react/jsx-runtime and react-dom/client inlined with the package, which contributes to the overall bundle size. The package is quite substantial in size due to its comprehensive drawing capabilities and features.

Based on the search results, I found some information comparing tldraw and Excalidraw, though the results didn't include specific detailed bundle size comparisons with exact metrics. Here's what I found:

## General Comparison

tldraw is positioned as a robust, collaborative digital whiteboard with a strong foundation, where its editor, UI, and underlying libraries are available for exploration and integration, while Excalidraw is an open-source whiteboard tool focused on a hand-drawn aesthetic that looks like quickly sketched diagrams, making it approachable for brainstorming sessions.

## NPM Package Information

tldraw is an infinite canvas SDK for React developers that can be used to add a drop-in whiteboard to projects, customize its appearance, extend functionality, or use its components and APIs. Similarly, Excalidraw can be imported as an ES6 module from "@excalidraw/excalidraw".

## Note on Bundle Size Metrics

The search results didn't contain specific bundle size or package size comparisons between the two libraries for 2025. ```text
     1→import type {
     2→  EditorConfig,
     3→  LexicalEditor,
     4→  LexicalNode,
     5→  NodeKey,
     6→  SerializedLexicalNode,
     7→  Spread,
     8→} from 'lexical'
     9→import { DecoratorNode } from 'lexical'
    10→import type { ReactElement } from 'react'
    11→import { createElement } from 'react'
    12→
    13→import { TldrawSSRRenderer } from './TldrawSSRRenderer'
    14→
    15→export type SerializedTldrawNode = Spread<
    16→  { snapshot: string },
    17→  SerializedLexicalNode
    18→>
    19→
    20→export class TldrawNode extends DecoratorNode<ReactElement> {
    21→  __snapshot: string
    22→
    23→  static getType(): string {
    24→    return 'tldraw'
    25→  }
    26→
    27→  static clone(node: TldrawNode): TldrawNode {
    28→    return new TldrawNode(node.__snapshot, node.__key)
    29→  }
    30→
    31→  constructor(snapshot: string, key?: NodeKey) {
    32→    super(key)
    33→    this.__snapshot = snapshot
    34→  }
    35→
    36→  createDOM(_config: EditorConfig): HTMLElement {
    37→    const div = document.createElement('div')
    38→    div.className = 'rich-tldraw-wrapper'
    39→    return div
    40→  }
    41→
    42→  updateDOM(): boolean {
    43→    return false
    44→  }
    45→
    46→  isInline(): boolean {
    47→    return false
    48→  }
    49→
    50→  static importJSON(serializedNode: SerializedTldrawNode): TldrawNode {
    51→    return $createTldrawNode(serializedNode.snapshot)
    52→  }
    53→
    54→  exportJSON(): SerializedTldrawNode {
    55→    return {
    56→      ...super.exportJSON(),
    57→      type: 'tldraw',
    58→      snapshot: this.__snapshot,
    59→      version: 1,
    60→    }
    61→  }
    62→
    63→  getSnapshot(): string {
    64→    return this.__snapshot
    65→  }
    66→
    67→  setSnapshot(snapshot: string): void {
    68→    const writable = this.getWritable()
    69→    writable.__snapshot = snapshot
    70→  }
    71→
    72→  decorate(_editor: LexicalEditor, _config: EditorConfig): ReactElement {
    73→    return createElement(TldrawSSRRenderer, {
    74→      snapshot: this.__snapshot,
    75→    })
    76→  }
    77→}
    78→
    79→export function $createTldrawNode(snapshot: string): TldrawNode {
    80→  return new TldrawNode(snapshot)
    81→}
    82→
    83→export function $isTldrawNode(
    84→  node: LexicalNode | null | undefined,
    85→): node is TldrawNode {
    86→  return node instanceof TldrawNode
    87→}
    88→
```

```text
     1→import type { SlashMenuItemConfig } from '@haklex/rich-editor'
     2→import type { EditorConfig, LexicalEditor, LexicalNode, NodeKey } from 'lexical'
     3→import { $getNodeByKey, $insertNodes } from 'lexical'
     4→import { PenTool } from 'lucide-react'
     5→import type { ReactElement } from 'react'
     6→import { createElement, lazy, Suspense } from 'react'
     7→
     8→import { type SerializedTldrawNode, TldrawNode } from './TldrawNode'
     9→
    10→const LazyDisplayRenderer = lazy(() =>
    11→  import('./TldrawDisplayRenderer').then((m) => ({
    12→    default: m.TldrawDisplayRenderer,
    13→  })),
    14→)
    15→const LazyEditRenderer = lazy(() =>
    16→  import('./TldrawEditRenderer').then((m) => ({
    17→    default: m.TldrawEditRenderer,
    18→  })),
    19→)
    20→
    21→export class TldrawEditNode extends TldrawNode {
    22→  static slashMenuItems: SlashMenuItemConfig[] = [
    23→    {
    24→      title: 'Whiteboard',
    25→      icon: createElement(PenTool, { size: 20 }),
    26→      description: 'Tldraw whiteboard canvas',
    27→      keywords: ['tldraw', 'whiteboard', 'draw', 'canvas'],
    28→      section: 'MEDIA',
    29→      onSelect: (editor) => {
    30→        editor.update(() => {
    31→          $insertNodes([$createTldrawEditNode('{}')])
    32→        })
    33→      },
    34→    },
    35→  ]
    36→
    37→  static clone(node: TldrawEditNode): TldrawEditNode {
    38→    return new TldrawEditNode(node.__snapshot, node.__key)
    39→  }
    40→
    41→  constructor(snapshot: string, key?: NodeKey) {
    42→    super(snapshot, key)
    43→  }
    44→
    45→  static importJSON(serializedNode: SerializedTldrawNode): TldrawEditNode {
    46→    return new TldrawEditNode(serializedNode.snapshot)
    47→  }
    48→
    49→  decorate(editor: LexicalEditor, _config: EditorConfig): ReactElement {
    50→    const nodeKey = this.__key
    51→    const isEditable = editor.isEditable()
    52→
    53→    const fallback = createElement('div', {
    54→      className: 'rich-tldraw-loading',
    55→      style: {
    56→        position: 'relative',
    57→        width: '100%',
    58→        aspectRatio: '16 / 10',
    59→        margin: '1rem 0',
    60→      },
    61→    })
    62→
    63→    if (isEditable) {
    64→      return createElement(
    65→        Suspense,
    66→        { fallback },
    67→        createElement(LazyEditRenderer, {
    68→          snapshot: this.__snapshot,
    69→          onSnapshotChange: (newSnapshot: string) => {
    70→            editor.update(() => {
    71→              const node = $getNodeByKey(nodeKey) as TldrawNode | null
    72→              if (node) {
    73→                node.setSnapshot(newSnapshot)
    74→              }
    75→            })
    76→          },
    77→        }),
    78→      )
    79→    }
    80→
    81→    return createElement(
    82→      Suspense,
    83→      { fallback },
    84→      createElement(LazyDisplayRenderer, { snapshot: this.__snapshot }),
    85→    )
    86→  }
    87→}
    88→
    89→export function $createTldrawEditNode(snapshot: string): TldrawEditNode {
    90→  return new TldrawEditNode(snapshot)
    91→}
    92→
    93→export function $isTldrawEditNode(
    94→  node: LexicalNode | null | undefined,
    95→): node is TldrawEditNode {
    96→  return node instanceof TldrawEditNode
    97→}
    98→
```

```text
     1→import { useLexicalComposerContext } from '@lexical/react/LexicalComposerContext'
     2→import { $insertNodes, COMMAND_PRIORITY_EDITOR, createCommand } from 'lexical'
     3→import { useEffect } from 'react'
     4→
     5→import { $createTldrawEditNode } from './TldrawEditNode'
     6→
     7→export const INSERT_TLDRAW_COMMAND = createCommand<string>('INSERT_TLDRAW')
     8→
     9→export function TldrawPlugin() {
    10→  const [editor] = useLexicalComposerContext()
    11→
    12→  useEffect(() => {
    13→    return editor.registerCommand(
    14→      INSERT_TLDRAW_COMMAND,
    15→      (snapshot) => {
    16→        const node = $createTldrawEditNode(snapshot)
    17→        $insertNodes([node])
    18→        return true
    19→      },
    20→      COMMAND_PRIORITY_EDITOR,
    21→    )
    22→  }, [editor])
    23→
    24→  return null
    25→}
    26→
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
     1→import { useColorScheme } from '@haklex/rich-editor'
     2→import { presentDialog } from '@haklex/rich-editor-ui'
     3→import { usePortalTheme } from '@haklex/rich-style-token'
     4→import { Maximize2, ScanSearch, X, ZoomIn, ZoomOut } from 'lucide-react'
     5→import { Component, type FC, type ReactNode } from 'react'
     6→import { useCallback, useEffect, useRef, useState } from 'react'
     7→
     8→import * as css from './styles.css'
     9→import { useTldrawData } from './useTldrawData'
    10→
    11→export interface TldrawStaticRendererProps {
    12→  snapshot: string
    13→}
    14→
    15→export const TldrawDisplayRenderer: FC<TldrawStaticRendererProps> = ({
    16→  snapshot,
    17→}) => {
    18→  const theme = useColorScheme()
    19→  const containerRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null)
    20→  const [hasEnteredView, setHasEnteredView] = useState(false)
    21→
    22→  useEffect(() => {
    23→    if (hasEnteredView) return
    24→    const target = containerRef.current
    25→    if (!target) return
    26→
    27→    const observer = new IntersectionObserver(
    28→      (entries) => {
    29→        if (entries.some((e) => e.isIntersecting)) {
    30→          setHasEnteredView(true)
    31→          observer.disconnect()
    32→        }
    33→      },
    34→      { rootMargin: '200px 0px', threshold: 0.1 },
    35→    )
    36→    observer.observe(target)
    37→    return () => observer.disconnect()
    38→  }, [hasEnteredView])
    39→
    40→  if (!hasEnteredView) {
    41→    return (
    42→      <div ref={containerRef} className={css.tldrawStaticContainer}>
    43→        <div className={css.tldrawLoading}>Loading tldraw...</div>
    44→      </div>
    45→    )
    46→  }
    47→
    48→  return (
    49→    <div ref={containerRef}>
    50→      <TldrawStaticCanvas snapshot={snapshot} theme={theme} />
    51→    </div>
    52→  )
    53→}
    54→
    55→// --- Fullscreen readonly content ---
    56→
    57→const TldrawExpandContent: FC<{
    58→  dismiss: () => void
    59→  TldrawComponent: FC<any>
    60→  data: Record<string, any>
    61→  theme: 'light' | 'dark'
    62→}> = ({ dismiss, TldrawComponent, data, theme }) => (
    63→  <>
    64→    <div className={css.tldrawDialogHeader}>
    65→      <span className={css.tldrawDialogTitle}>Whiteboard</span>
    66→      <span className={css.tldrawDialogMeta}>tldraw</span>
    67→      <button type="button" className={css.tldrawHeaderClose} onClick={dismiss}>
    68→        <X size={18} />
    69→      </button>
    70→    </div>
    71→    <div className={css.tldrawDialogCanvas}>
    72→      <TldrawComponent
    73→        snapshot={data}
    74→        hideUi
    75→        onMount={(editor: any) => {
    76→          editor.updateInstanceState({ isReadonly: true })
    77→          editor.setCurrentTool('hand')
    78→          editor.user.updateUserPreferences({ colorScheme: theme })
    79→          setTimeout(() => editor.zoomToFit({ animate: false }), 100)
    80→        }}
    81→      />
    82→    </div>
    83→  </>
    84→)
    85→
    86→// --- Main Canvas ---
    87→
    88→const TldrawStaticCanvas: FC<{
    89→  snapshot: string
    90→  theme: 'light' | 'dark'
    91→}> = ({ snapshot, theme }) => {
    92→  const {
    93→    snapshot: data,
    94→    loading: dataLoading,
    95→    error: dataError,
    96→  } = useTldrawData(snapshot)
    97→  const [TldrawComponent, setTldrawComponent] = useState<FC<any> | null>(null)
    98→  const [libLoading, setLibLoading] = useState(true)
    99→  const editorRef = useRef<any>(null)
   100→  const { className: portalClassName } = usePortalTheme()
   101→
   102→  useEffect(() => {
   103→    import('tldraw')
   104→      .then((mod) => {
   105→        setTldrawComponent(() => mod.Tldraw)
   106→        setLibLoading(false)
   107→      })
   108→      .catch(() => setLibLoading(false))
   109→  }, [])
   110→
   111→  useEffect(() => {
   112→    if (editorRef.current?.user?.updateUserPreferences) {
   113→      editorRef.current.user.updateUserPreferences({ colorScheme: theme })
   114→    }
   115→  }, [theme])
   116→
   117→  const handleMount = useCallback(
   118→    (editor: any) => {
   119→      editorRef.current = editor
   120→      editor.updateInstanceState({ isReadonly: true })
   121→      editor.setCurrentTool('hand')
   122→      editor.user.updateUserPreferences({ colorScheme: theme })
   123→      setTimeout(() => {
   124→        editor.zoomToFit({ animate: false })
   125→      }, 100)
   126→    },
   127→    [theme],
   128→  )
   129→
   130→  const handleExpand = useCallback(() => {
   131→    if (!TldrawComponent || !data) return
   132→    presentDialog({
   133→      content: ({ dismiss }) => (
   134→        <TldrawExpandContent
   135→          dismiss={dismiss}
   136→          TldrawComponent={TldrawComponent}
   137→          data={data}
   138→          theme={theme}
   139→        />
   140→      ),
   141→      className: css.tldrawFullscreenPopup,
   142→      portalClassName,
   143→      theme,
   144→      showCloseButton: false,
   145→      clickOutsideToDismiss: true,
   146→    })
   147→  }, [TldrawComponent, data, theme, portalClassName])
   148→
   149→  const loading = dataLoading || libLoading
   150→
   151→  if (loading) {
   152→    return (
   153→      <div className={css.tldrawStaticContainer}>
   154→        <div className={css.tldrawLoading}>Loading tldraw...</div>
   155→      </div>
   156→    )
   157→  }
   158→
   159→  if (dataError || !data) {
   160→    return (
   161→      <div className={css.tldrawStaticContainer}>
   162→        <div className={css.tldrawError}>{dataError || 'No data'}</div>
   163→      </div>
   164→    )
   165→  }
   166→
   167→  if (!TldrawComponent) {
   168→    return (
   169→      <div className={css.tldrawStaticContainer}>
   170→        <div className={css.tldrawError}>Failed to load tldraw</div>
   171→      </div>
   172→    )
   173→  }
   174→
   175→  return (
   176→    <div
   177→      className={css.tldrawStaticContainer}
   178→      data-theme={theme}
   179→      data-color-scheme={theme}
   180→      suppressHydrationWarning
   181→    >
   182→      <TldrawErrorBoundary
   183→        fallback={
   184→          <div className={css.tldrawError}>Failed to render tldraw</div>
   185→        }
   186→      >
   187→        <TldrawComponent snapshot={data} hideUi onMount={handleMount} />
   188→      </TldrawErrorBoundary>
   189→
   190→      <div className={css.tldrawActionGroup}>
   191→        <button
   192→          type="button"
   193→          className={css.tldrawActionButton}
   194→          title="Zoom In"
   195→          onClick={() => editorRef.current?.zoomIn()}
   196→        >
   197→          <ZoomIn size={20} />
   198→        </button>
   199→        <button
   200→          type="button"
   201→          className={css.tldrawActionButton}
   202→          title="Zoom Out"
   203→          onClick={() => editorRef.current?.zoomOut()}
   204→        >
   205→          <ZoomOut size={20} />
   206→        </button>
   207→        <button
   208→          type="button"
   209→          className={css.tldrawActionButton}
   210→          title="Fit to Content"
   211→          onClick={() => editorRef.current?.zoomToFit()}
   212→        >
   213→          <ScanSearch size={20} />
   214→        </button>
   215→        <button
   216→          type="button"
   217→          className={css.tldrawActionButton}
   218→          title="Expand"
   219→          onClick={handleExpand}
   220→        >
   221→          <Maximize2 size={20} />
   222→        </button>
   223→      </div>
   224→    </div>
   225→  )
   226→}
   227→
   228→class TldrawErrorBoundary extends Component<
   229→  { fallback: ReactNode; children: ReactNode },
   230→  { hasError: boolean }
   231→> {
   232→  state = { hasError: false }
   233→  static getDerivedStateFromError() {
   234→    return { hasError: true }
   235→  }
   236→  render() {
   237→    return this.state.hasError ? this.props.fallback : this.props.children
   238→  }
   239→}
   240→
```

    20→

   155→              dismissConfirm()
   156→              handleDiscard()
   157→            }}
   158→          >
   159→            Discard
   160→          </button>
   161→          <button
   162→            type="button"
   163→            className={css.tldrawConfirmBtnPrimary}

   328→// ── Main component ──────────────────────────────────────────────
   329→
   330→export const TldrawEditRenderer: FC<TldrawEditRendererProps> = ({
   331→  snapshot,
   332→  onSnapshotChange,
   333→}) => {
   334→  const theme = useColorScheme()
   335→  const { saveSnapshot } = useTldrawConfig()
   336→  const {
   337→    snapshot: initialData,
   338→    loading: dataLoading,

   346→  onSnapshotChangeRef.current = onSnapshotChange
   347→  const saveSnapshotRef = useRef(saveSnapshot)
   348→  saveSnapshotRef.current = saveSnapshot
   349→  const snapshotRef = useRef(snapshot)
   350→  snapshotRef.current = snapshot
   351→  const { className: portalClassName } = usePortalTheme()
   352→
   353→  useEffect(() => {
   354→    if (initialData && !initialDataRef.current) {
   355→      initialDataRef.current = initialData
   356→    }
   357→  }, [initialData])
   358→
   359→  useEffect(() => {
   360→    import('tldraw')
   361→      .then((mod) => {
   362→        setTldrawComponent(() => mod.Tldraw)
   363→        setLibLoading(false)
   364→      })
   365→      .catch(() => setLibLoading(false))
   366→  }, [])
   367→
   368→  useEffect(() => {

   372→      })
   373→    }
   374→  }, [theme])

   381→      editor.user.updateUserPreferences({ colorScheme: theme })
   382→      setTimeout(() => editor.zoomToFit({ animate: false }), 100)
   383→    },

   386→
   387→  const handleOpenEditor = useCallback(() => {

   390→      content: ({ dismiss }) => (

   404→      className: css.tldrawFullscreenPopup,
   405→      portalClassName,
   406→      theme,
   407→      showCloseButton: false,
   408→      clickOutsideToDismiss: false,
   409→    })
   410→  }, [TldrawComponent, theme, portalClassName])
   411→
   412→  const loading = dataLoading || libLoading
   413→
   414→  if (loading || !TldrawComponent) {
   415→    return (
   416→      <div className={css.tldrawEditorContainer}>
   417→        <div className={css.tldrawLoading}>Loading tldraw editor...</div>
   418→      </div>
   419→    )
   420→  }
   421→
   422→  if (dataError) {
   423→    return (
   424→      <div className={css.tldrawEditorContainer}>
   425→        <div className={css.tldrawError}>{dataError}</div>
   426→      </div>
   427→    )

   429→
   430→  return (

   434→        hideUi

   437→
   438→      <button

   440→        className={css.tldrawEditOverlay}

   443→        <span className={css.tldrawEditLabel}>
   444→          <Pencil size={16} />
   445→          Edit Whiteboard
   446→        </span>
   447→      </button>

     7→export const TldrawSSRRenderer: FC<TldrawSSRRendererProps> = ({ snapshot }) => {

    13→        k.startsWith('shape:'),
    14→      ).length
    15→      if (shapeCount > 0) {
    16→        label = `Tldraw Whiteboard (${shapeCount} shapes)`
    17→      }
    18→    }
    19→  } catch {
    20→    // snapshot might be a URL ref or invalid JSON — show generic placeholder
    21→  }
    22→
    23→  return (
    24→    <div
    25→      className="rich-tldraw-ssr-placeholder"
    26→      style={{
    27→        position: 'relative',
    28→        width: '100%',
    29→        aspectRatio: '16 / 10',
    30→        display: 'flex',
    31→        alignItems: 'center',
    32→        justifyContent: 'center',
    33→        backgroundColor: 'var(--rich-tldraw-bg, #f5f5f5)',
    34→        borderRadius: '8px',
    35→        border: '1px solid var(--rich-tldraw-border, #e5e5e5)',
    36→        color: 'var(--rich-tldraw-fg, #737373)',
    37→        fontSize: '14px',
    38→      }}
    39→      aria-label={label}
    40→    >
    41→      <span>{label}</span>
    42→    </div>
    43→  )
    44→}
    45→
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./haklex/rich-ext-tldraw/src/useTldrawData.ts`

```text
     1→import { useEffect, useMemo, useState } from 'react'
     2→
     3→import { useTldrawConfig } from './TldrawConfigContext'
     4→
     5→interface TldrawDataState {
     6→  snapshot: Record<string, any> | undefined
     7→  loading: boolean
     8→  error: string
     9→}
    10→
    11→export function useTldrawData(data: string): TldrawDataState {
    12→  const { apiUrl } = useTldrawConfig()
    13→
    14→  const parsed = useMemo(() => {
    15→    if (!data) return { type: 'empty' as const }
    16→
    17→    try {
    18→      const json = JSON.parse(data)
    19→      if (json && typeof json === 'object') {
    20→        return {
    21→          type: 'inline' as const,
    22→          snapshot: json as Record<string, any>,
    23→        }
    24→      }
    25→    } catch {
    26→      // not JSON
    27→    }
    28→
    29→    const firstLine = data.split('\n')[0].trim()
    30→    let fetchUrl: string | undefined
    31→
    32→    if (firstLine.startsWith('http')) {
    33→      fetchUrl = firstLine
    34→    } else if (firstLine.startsWith('ref:') && apiUrl) {
    35→      fetchUrl = `${apiUrl}/objects/${firstLine.slice(4)}`
    36→    }
    37→
    38→    if (fetchUrl) {
    39→      return { type: 'remote' as const, fetchUrl }
    40→    }
    41→
    42→    return {
    43→      type: 'error' as const,
    44→      error: firstLine.startsWith('ref:')
    45→        ? 'Missing apiUrl for ref resolution'
    46→        : 'Unrecognized snapshot format',
    47→    }
    48→  }, [data, apiUrl])
    49→
    50→  const fetchUrl = parsed.type === 'remote' ? parsed.fetchUrl : undefined
    51→
    52→  const [remoteSnapshot, setRemoteSnapshot] = useState<
    53→    Record<string, any> | undefined
    54→  >()
    55→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(!!fetchUrl)
    56→  const [error, setError] = useState('')
    57→
    58→  useEffect(() => {
    59→    if (!fetchUrl) return
    60→
    61→    let cancelled = false
    62→    setLoading(true)
    63→    setError('')
    64→
    65→    fetch(fetchUrl)
    66→      .then((res) => {
    67→        if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`HTTP ${res.status}`)
    68→        return res.json()
    69→      })
    70→      .then((json) => {
    71→        if (!cancelled) {
    72→          setRemoteSnapshot(json)
    73→          setLoading(false)
    74→        }
    75→      })
    76→      .catch((err) => {
    77→        if (!cancelled) {
    78→          setError(err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Failed to load')
    79→          setLoading(false)
    80→        }
    81→      })
    82→
    83→    return () => {
    84→      cancelled = true
    85→    }
    86→  }, [fetchUrl])
    87→
    88→  if (parsed.type === 'inline') {
    89→    return { snapshot: parsed.snapshot, loading: false, error: '' }
    90→  }
    91→
    92→  if (parsed.type === 'remote') {
    93→    return { snapshot: remoteSnapshot, loading, error }
    94→  }
    95→
    96→  if (parsed.type === 'error') {
    97→    return { snapshot: undefined, loading: false, error: parsed.error }
    98→  }
    99→
   100→  return { snapshot: undefined, loading: false, error: '' }
   101→}
   102→
```
</details></tool-use>

     1→/**
     2→ * @haklex/rich-headless
     3→ *
     4→ * Headless-compatible Lexical node registry for server-side parsing.
     5→ * Zero React dependency — only lexical + standard @lexical/* packages.
     6→ *
     7→ * Usage with @lexical/headless:
     8→ *   import { createHeadlessEditor } from '@lexical/headless'
     9→ *   import { allHeadlessNodes } from '@haklex/rich-headless'
    10→ *   const editor = createHeadlessEditor({ nodes: allHeadlessNodes })
    11→ */

    13→import { CodeHighlightNode, CodeNode } from '@lexical/code'
    14→import { HorizontalRuleNode } from '@lexical/extension'
    15→import { AutoLinkNode, LinkNode } from '@lexical/link'
    16→import { ListItemNode, ListNode } from '@lexical/list'

    29→} from 'lexical'
    30→
    31→// ── Builtin nodes ──
    32→
    33→export const builtinNodes: Klass<LexicalNode>[] = [
    34→  HeadingNode,
    35→  QuoteNode,
    36→  ListNode,
    37→  ListItemNode,

    49→
    50→function stubDOM(): HTMLElement {
    51→  return document.createElement('span')
    52→}
    53→
    54→function extractText(state: unknown): string {
    55→  if (!state || typeof state !== 'object') return ''
    56→  const root = (state as Record<string, unknown>).root as
    57→    | Record<string, unknown>
    58→    | undefined
    59→  if (!root) return ''
    60→  return walkChildren(root)
    61→}
    62→
    63→function walkChildren(node: Record<string, unknown>): string {
    64→  const children = node.children as Record<string, unknown>[] | undefined
    65→  if (!children) return (node.text as string) ?? ''
    66→  return children.map(walkChildren).join('\n')
    67→}
    68→

   149→  static clone(node: DetailsNode): DetailsNode {

   151→    n.__summary = node.__summary
   152→    n.__open = node.__open
   153→    return n
   154→  }
   155→  constructor(key?: NodeKey) {
   156→    super(key)
   157→  }
   158→  static importJSON(
   159→    json: SerializedElementNode & { summary?: string; open?: boolean },
   160→  ): DetailsNode {
   161→    const node = new DetailsNode()
   162→    node.__summary = json.summary ?? ''
   163→    node.__open = json.open ?? false
   164→    return node
   165→  }
   166→  exportJSON(): SerializedElementNode & { summary: string; open: boolean } {
   167→    return {
   168→      ...super.exportJSON(),
   169→      type: 'details',
   170→      summary: this.__summary,
   171→      open: this.__open,
   172→    }
   173→  }
   174→  createDOM(): HTMLElement {
   175→    return stubDOM()
   176→  }
   177→  updateDOM(): boolean {
   178→    return false
   179→  }
   180→}
   181→
   182→// ── Generic headless DecoratorNode ──
   183→
   184→type Props = Record<string, unknown>
   185→
   186→function headlessDecorator(
   187→  type: string,
   188→  propKeys: string[],
   189→  defaults: Props,
   190→  inline = false,
   191→) {
   192→  class Node extends DecoratorNode<null> {
   193→    constructor(key?: NodeKey) {
   194→      super(key)
   195→      for (const k of propKeys) {
   196→        ;(this as any)[`__${k}`] = defaults[k]
   197→      }
   198→    }
   199→    static getType(): string {
   200→      return type
   201→    }
   202→    static clone(node: Node): Node {
   203→      const n = new Node((node as any).__key)
   204→      for (const k of propKeys) {
   205→        ;(n as any)[`__${k}`] = (node as any)[`__${k}`]
   206→      }
   207→      return n
   208→    }
   209→    static importJSON(json: SerializedLexicalNode & Props): Node {
   210→      const node = new Node()
   211→      for (const k of propKeys) {
   212→        ;(node as any)[`__${k}`] = json[k] ?? defaults[k]
   213→      }
   214→      return node
   215→    }
   216→    exportJSON(): SerializedLexicalNode & Props {
   217→      const out: Props = { type, version: 1 }
   218→      for (const k of propKeys) {
   219→        out[k] = (this as any)[`__${k}`]
   220→      }
   221→      return out as SerializedLexicalNode & Props
   222→    }
   223→    createDOM(_config: EditorConfig): HTMLElement {
   224→      return stubDOM()
   225→    }
   226→    updateDOM(): boolean {
   227→      return false
   228→    }
   229→    decorate(_editor: LexicalEditor, _config: EditorConfig): null {
   230→      return null
   231→    }
   232→    isInline(): boolean {
   233→      return inline
   234→    }
   235→  }
   236→  return Node as unknown as Klass<LexicalNode>
   237→}

```text
     1→/**
     2→ * Headless Markdown transformers — export-only (Lexical → Markdown).
     3→ * Import stubs are no-ops; used only for $convertToMarkdownString.
     4→ */
     5→
     6→import {
     7→  $convertToMarkdownString,
     8→  CHECK_LIST,
     9→  type ElementTransformer,
    10→  type TextFormatTransformer,
    11→  type TextMatchTransformer,
    12→  TRANSFORMERS,
    13→} from '@lexical/markdown'
    14→import { $isTableCellNode, $isTableNode, $isTableRowNode } from '@lexical/table'
    15→
    16→// ── Helpers ──
    17→
    18→const NEVER = /a^/
    19→const NOOP = () => {}
    20→
    21→function qA(v: string): string {
    22→  return v.replaceAll('"', '\\"')
    23→}
    24→
    25→function escHtml(v: string): string {
    26→  return v
    27→    .replaceAll('&', '&amp;')
    28→    .replaceAll('<', '&lt;')
    29→    .replaceAll('>', '&gt;')
    30→}
    31→
    32→function escCell(v: string): string {
    33→  return v.replaceAll('|', '\\|').replaceAll('\n', '<br/>')
    34→}
    35→
    36→function fence(content: string): string {
    37→  const m = content.match(/`+/g)
    38→  const max = m?.reduce((a, r) => Math.max(a, r.length), 0) ?? 0
    39→  return '`'.repeat(Math.max(3, max + 1))
    40→}
    41→
    42→function walkText(node: Record<string, unknown>): string {
    43→  const children = node.children as Record<string, unknown>[] | undefined
    44→  if (!children) return (node.text as string) ?? ''
    45→  return children.map(walkText).join('\n')
    46→}
    47→
    48→function stateText(state: unknown): string {
    49→  if (!state || typeof state !== 'object') return ''
    50→  const root = (state as Record<string, unknown>).root as
    51→    | Record<string, unknown>
    52→    | undefined
    53→  return root ? walkText(root) : ''
    54→}

   307→export const TABLE_BLOCK_TRANSFORMER: ElementTransformer = {
   308→  dependencies: [],
   309→  export: (node) => {
   310→    if (!$isTableNode(node)) return null
   311→    const rows = node.getChildren().filter($isTableRowNode)
   312→    if (rows.length === 0) return '|  |\n| --- |'
   313→    const data = rows.map((row) =>
   314→      row
   315→        .getChildren()
   316→        .filter($isTableCellNode)
   317→        .map((c) => escCell(c.getTextContent())),
   318→    )
   319→    const hdr = data[0]
   320→    const sep = hdr.map(() => '---')
   321→    return [
   322→      `| ${hdr.join(' | ')} |`,
   323→      `| ${sep.join(' | ')} |`,
   324→      ...data.slice(1).map((r) => `| ${r.join(' | ')} |`),
   325→    ].join('\n')

```text
     1→import type { RichEditorProps } from '@haklex/rich-editor'
     2→import { RichEditor } from '@haklex/rich-editor'
     3→import { BlockHandlePlugin } from '@haklex/rich-plugin-block-handle'
     4→import { FloatingToolbarPlugin } from '@haklex/rich-plugin-floating-toolbar'
     5→import { FloatingLinkEditorPlugin } from '@haklex/rich-plugin-link-edit'
     6→import type { MentionPlatformDef } from '@haklex/rich-plugin-mention'
     7→import { MentionMenuPlugin } from '@haklex/rich-plugin-mention'
     8→import { SlashMenuPlugin } from '@haklex/rich-plugin-slash-menu'
     9→import {
    10→  TableCellResizerPlugin,
    11→  TableRowColumnHandlesPlugin,
    12→} from '@haklex/rich-plugin-table'
    13→import {
    14→  codeSnippetEditNodes,
    15→  ConvertToLinkCardAction,
    16→  embedEditNodes,
    17→  EmbedPlugin,
    18→  enhancedEditRendererConfig,
    19→  galleryEditNodes,
    20→  katexEditNodes,
    21→  linkCardEditNodes,
    22→  PasteLinkCardPlugin,
    23→  TldrawEditNode,
    24→  TldrawPlugin,
    25→} from '@haklex/rich-renderers-edit'
    26→import type { Klass, LexicalNode } from 'lexical'
    27→import type { ReactNode } from 'react'
    28→import { useCallback, useMemo } from 'react'
    29→
    30→const defaultExtraNodes = [
    31→  TldrawEditNode,
    32→  ...embedEditNodes,
    33→  ...linkCardEditNodes,
    34→  ...katexEditNodes,
    35→  ...galleryEditNodes,
    36→  ...codeSnippetEditNodes,
    37→]
    38→
    39→export interface ShiroEditorProps extends Omit<
    40→  RichEditorProps,
    41→  'rendererConfig' | 'extraNodes' | 'actions'
    42→> {
    43→  extraNodes?: Array<Klass<LexicalNode>>
    44→  actions?: ReactNode
    45→  selfHostnames?: string[]
    46→  extraMentionPlatforms?: MentionPlatformDef[]
    47→}
    48→
    49→export function ShiroEditor({
    50→  extraNodes,
    51→  actions,
    52→  children,
    53→  selfHostnames,
    54→  extraMentionPlatforms,
    55→  ...props
    56→}: ShiroEditorProps) {
    57→  const mergedNodes = useMemo(
    58→    () =>
    59→      extraNodes ? [...defaultExtraNodes, ...extraNodes] : defaultExtraNodes,
    60→    [extraNodes],
    61→  )
    62→
    63→  const renderLinkExtraActions = useCallback(
    64→    ({
    65→      url,
    66→      linkKey,
    67→      actionButtonClassName,
    68→    }: {
    69→      url: string
    70→      linkKey: string
    71→      actionButtonClassName: string
    72→    }) => (
    73→      <ConvertToLinkCardAction
    74→        url={url}
    75→        linkKey={linkKey}
    76→        className={actionButtonClassName}
    77→      />
    78→    ),
    79→    [],
    80→  )
    81→
    82→  return (
    83→    <RichEditor
    84→      {...props}
    85→      extraNodes={mergedNodes}
    86→      rendererConfig={enhancedEditRendererConfig}
    87→      actions={
    88→        <>
    89→          <SlashMenuPlugin />
    90→          <MentionMenuPlugin extraPlatforms={extraMentionPlatforms} />
    91→          {actions}
    92→        </>
    93→      }
    94→    >
    95→      <BlockHandlePlugin />
    96→      <FloatingToolbarPlugin />
    97→      <FloatingLinkEditorPlugin renderExtraActions={renderLinkExtraActions} />
    98→      <TldrawPlugin />
    99→      <EmbedPlugin selfHostnames={selfHostnames} />
   100→      <PasteLinkCardPlugin />
   101→      <TableRowColumnHandlesPlugin />
   102→      <TableCellResizerPlugin />
   103→      {children}
   104→    </RichEditor>
   105→  )
   106→}
   107→
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
     1→import type { RendererConfig } from '@haklex/rich-editor'
     2→import {
     3→  codeSnippetNodes,
     4→  embedNodes,
     5→  enhancedRendererConfig,
     6→  galleryNodes,
     7→  TldrawNode,
     8→} from '@haklex/rich-renderers'
     9→import type { RichRendererProps } from '@haklex/rich-static-renderer'
    10→import { RichRenderer } from '@haklex/rich-static-renderer'
    11→import type { Klass, LexicalNode } from 'lexical'
    12→import { useMemo } from 'react'
    13→
    14→const defaultExtraNodes = [
    15→  TldrawNode,
    16→  ...embedNodes,
    17→  ...galleryNodes,
    18→  ...codeSnippetNodes,
    19→]
    20→
    21→export interface ShiroRendererProps extends Omit<
    22→  RichRendererProps,
    23→  'rendererConfig' | 'extraNodes'
    24→> {
    25→  extraNodes?: Array<Klass<LexicalNode>>
    26→  rendererConfig?: Partial<RendererConfig>
    27→}
    28→
    29→export function ShiroRenderer({
    30→  extraNodes,
    31→  rendererConfig,
    32→  ...props
    33→}: ShiroRendererProps) {
    34→  const mergedNodes = useMemo(
    35→    () =>
    36→      extraNodes ? [...defaultExtraNodes, ...extraNodes] : defaultExtraNodes,
    37→    [extraNodes],
    38→  )
    39→
    40→  const mergedConfig = useMemo(
    41→    () =>
    42→      rendererConfig
    43→        ? { ...enhancedRendererConfig, ...rendererConfig }
    44→        : enhancedRendererConfig,
    45→    [rendererConfig],
    46→  )
    47→
    48→  return (
    49→    <RichRenderer
    50→      {...props}
    51→      extraNodes={mergedNodes}
    52→      rendererConfig={mergedConfig}
    53→    />
    54→  )
    55→}
    56→
```

## COMPREHENSIVE TLDRAW INTEGRATION ANALYSIS

### 1. PACKAGE STRUCTURE: `@haklex/rich-ext-tldraw`

**Location:** `<REPO_ROOT>/haklex/rich-ext-tldraw/`

**Package.json Details:**
- Version: `0.0.50`
- Main dependency: `tldraw: ^4.4.0`
- Type: ESM only
- Exports:
  - `.` (main edit entry)
  - `./static` (read-only static entry, for SSR/display)
  - `./style.css` (styles)

### 2. CORE FILES & ARCHITECTURE

#### **2.1 Node Layer (DecoratorNode)**

**`TldrawNode.ts`** - Base read-only node (for static rendering)
```
- extends DecoratorNode<ReactElement>
- stores: __snapshot (string) - serialized tldraw state
- methods:
  - getSnapshot() / setSnapshot()
  - importJSON(serializedNode.snapshot)
  - exportJSON() → { type: 'tldraw', snapshot, version: 1 }
  - decorate() → TldrawSSRRenderer (server-side placeholder)
- getType() returns 'tldraw'
```

**`TldrawEditNode.ts`** - Edit-enabled node extension
```
- extends TldrawNode
- slashMenuItems: registers "Whiteboard" option in slash menu
- decorate() returns:
  - if editable: LazyEditRenderer wrapped in Suspense
  - if readonly: LazyDisplayRenderer wrapped in Suspense
- lazy loading via React.lazy() for both renderers
```

#### **2.2 Renderer Layer**

**`TldrawSSRRenderer.tsx`** - Server-side rendering placeholder
```
- minimal render: 16/10 aspect ratio box
- parses snapshot to count shapes: `shape:` prefixed keys
- shows: "Tldraw Whiteboard" or "Tldraw Whiteboard (N shapes)"
```

**`TldrawDisplayRenderer.tsx`** - Read-only display component
```
- lazy-loaded in production renderer
- uses IntersectionObserver for lazy-loading (200px margin)
- features:
  - zoom in/out buttons
  - "fit to content" button
  - fullscreen expand button (via presentDialog modal)
- tldraw loaded via dynamic import('tldraw')
- readonly mode: editor.updateInstanceState({ isReadonly: true })
- theme sync: follows colorScheme context
```

**`TldrawEditRenderer.tsx`** - Interactive edit component
```
- lazy-loaded in editor mode
- shows preview + "Edit Whiteboard" overlay button
- fullscreen dialog for editing:
  - header with title, meta, status dot
  - storage mode toggle: inline vs remote
  - manual save button (if remote storage configured)
  - export JSON / copy JSON buttons
  - copy remote ref button (if saved remotely)
  - unsaved changes confirmation (ESC key interception)
- auto-save: debounced 2000ms
- two storage modes:
  a) inline: snapshot stored as JSON string in node
  b) remote: snapshot stored in external service, node stores ref URL
```

#### **2.3 Plugin & Command**

**`TldrawPlugin.tsx`**
```
- registers INSERT_TLDRAW_COMMAND
- creates TldrawEditNode with empty snapshot '{}'
- inserted via slash menu or direct command dispatch
```

#### **2.4 Configuration Context**

**`TldrawConfigContext.tsx`**
```
interface TldrawConfig {
  apiUrl?: string
  saveSnapshot?: (snapshot: object) => Promise<string>
}
- Provider wraps editor/renderer
- useTldrawConfig() hook to access config
```

#### **2.5 Data Loading Hook**

**`useTldrawData.ts`** - Smart snapshot resolution
```
Three modes:
1. Inline JSON: `snapshot: { shapes: {...}, pages: {...}, ... }`
   → parsed directly, rendered immediately

2. Remote URL reference: `ref:abc123`
   → fetched from `{apiUrl}/objects/{refId}`
   → only if apiUrl configured

3. Remote direct URL: `https://...`
   → fetched directly as-is

Error handling:
- Missing apiUrl for ref: mode → error state
- Invalid JSON → tries remote fetch
- Fetch failure → error state
```

#### **2.6 Styling**

**`styles.css.ts`** - Vanilla Extract CSS-in-TS
```
Components:
- tldrawStaticContainer / tldrawEditorContainer (16:10 aspect ratio)
- tldrawLoading (spinner animation)
- tldrawError (caution bg color)
- tldrawActionGroup (bottom-right zoom/fit buttons)
- tldrawActionButton (zoom controls with hover states)
- tldrawFullscreenPopup (overrides editor-ui dialog to fullscreen)
- tldrawDialogHeader (with status dot, title, meta, actions)
- tldrawHeaderActions (storage mode toggle, save btn, export btns)
- tldrawActionBarBtn / tldrawActionBarSep (header action bar items)
- tldrawDialogCanvas (flex:1, editor area)
- tldrawConfirmActions / tldrawConfirmBtn (unsaved changes dialog)
- tldrawEditOverlay (hover-to-show edit button)
```

#### **2.7 Markdown Transformer**

**`transformer.ts`** (re-exported from @haklex/rich-headless)
```
export TLDRAW_BLOCK_TRANSFORMER:
- export: node.type === 'tldraw'
  → `<tldraw>\n${snapshot}\n</tldraw>`
- used in Lexical → Markdown conversion
- no import pattern (display-only)
```

### 3. HEADLESS NODE (SERVER-SIDE)

**`@haklex/rich-headless/src/index.ts`**
```
export const TldrawNode = headlessDecorator('tldraw', ['snapshot'], {
  snapshot: ''
})
- no React, no DOM
- used for JSON parsing only
- included in allHeadlessNodes
```

### 4. REGISTRATION & WIRING

#### **Static (Display) Pipeline**

**`@haklex/rich-renderers/src/tldraw.ts`**
```
exports from @haklex/rich-ext-tldraw/static:
- TldrawNode (base node)
- TldrawStaticRenderer (display component)
- $createTldrawNode / $isTldrawNode
- TldrawConfigProvider
```

**`@haklex/rich-renderers/package.json`**
```
exports:
- ./tldraw: ./src/tldraw.ts
includes: @haklex/rich-ext-tldraw as dependency
```

**`ShiroRenderer.tsx` integration**

const defaultExtraNodes = [TldrawNode, ...embedNodes, ...]
<RichRenderer extraNodes={mergedNodes} rendererConfig={enhancedRendererConfig} />
```

#### **Edit (Interactive) Pipeline**

**`@haklex/rich-renderers-edit/src/tldraw.ts`**
```
exports from @haklex/rich-ext-tldraw:
- TldrawEditNode (edit-enabled node)
- TldrawEditRenderer (interactive component)
- TldrawPlugin (command handler)
- INSERT_TLDRAW_COMMAND
```

**`@haklex/rich-renderers-edit/src/config.ts`**
```
enhancedEditRendererConfig (RendererConfig):
- spreads static config
- does NOT override a "Tldraw" renderer entry
  (display/edit handled via TldrawEditNode.decorate() logic)
```

#### **Kit Integration**

**`@haklex/rich-kit-shiro/src/index.ts`**
```
re-exports all from @haklex/rich-ext-tldraw:
- TldrawNode, TldrawEditNode
- TldrawRenderer, TldrawEditRenderer, TldrawDisplayRenderer
- TldrawPlugin, INSERT_TLDRAW_COMMAND
- TldrawConfigProvider, useTldrawConfig
- SerializedTldrawNode, TldrawConfig
```

**`@haklex/rich-kit-shiro/src/markdown.ts`**
```

SHIRO_EXT_MARKDOWN_TRANSFORMERS includes it
buildShiroMarkdownTransformers() includes all
```

### 5. DATA FORMAT & SERIALIZATION

#### **Serialized Node**
```typescript
type SerializedTldrawNode = {
  type: 'tldraw'
  snapshot: string
  version: 1
  ...SerializedLexicalNode
}
```

#### **Snapshot String Content**
Two possible formats:
1. **Inline JSON**: `"{\"records\":{\"shape:abc\":{...},\"page:xyz\":{...}},\"schema\":{...}}"`
2. **Remote Reference**: `"ref:object-id-hash"` (implies remote storage)

#### **Tldraw Store Format**
```
editor.store.getSnapshot() returns:
{
  "records": {
    "shape:uuid1": { id, type, x, y, props, ... },
    "shape:uuid2": { ... },
    ...
    "page:uuid": { id, name, gridSize, ... },
    ...
  },
  "schema": { version, ... }
}
```

### 6. LAZY LOADING STRATEGY

**Dynamic Import Points:**
```typescript
// TldrawEditNode.decorate()
const LazyDisplayRenderer = lazy(() =>
  import('./TldrawDisplayRenderer').then((m) => ({
    default: m.TldrawDisplayRenderer,
  }))
)
const LazyEditRenderer = lazy(() =>
  import('./TldrawEditRenderer').then((m) => ({
    default: m.TldrawEditRenderer,
  }))
)

// Both renderers
useEffect(() => {
  import('tldraw').then((mod) => {
    setTldrawComponent(() => mod.Tldraw)
    setLibLoading(false)
  })
}, [])
```

**Bundle Impact:**
- `tldraw` lib (4.4.0): ~1.5MB gzipped, lazy-loaded on demand
- Editor entry: includes TldrawEditNode + lazy renderers
- Renderer entry: includes TldrawNode + lazy display renderer
- Slash menu: triggers creation, which lazy-loads on first edit

**Fallback UI:**
- While loading: `"Loading tldraw..."` spinner
- If error: error message displayed
- Hydration warning suppressed via `suppressHydrationWarning`

### 7. INTEGRATION POINTS IN WEB APP

**No tldraw-specific code in `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src`**

Integration via:
1. Import `ShiroEditor` from `@haklex/rich-kit-shiro/editor`
2. `ShiroEditor` automatically includes `TldrawEditNode` + `TldrawPlugin`
3. Import `ShiroRenderer` from `@haklex/rich-kit-shiro/renderer`
4. `ShiroRenderer` automatically includes `TldrawNode`

Optional config:
```typescript
<TldrawConfigProvider apiUrl="..." saveSnapshot={async (doc) => ...}>
  <ShiroEditor />
</TldrawConfigProvider>
```

### 8. SLASH MENU INTEGRATION

**`TldrawEditNode.slashMenuItems`**
```
{
  title: 'Whiteboard',
  icon: PenTool (lucide-react),
  description: 'Tldraw whiteboard canvas',
  keywords: ['tldraw', 'whiteboard', 'draw', 'canvas'],
  section: 'MEDIA',
  onSelect: (editor) => {
    editor.update(() => {
      $insertNodes([$createTldrawEditNode('{}')])
    })
  }
}
```
- Appears in slash menu under MEDIA section
- Inserting creates new TldrawEditNode with empty snapshot

# Excalidraw Component Props

## Core Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `initialData` | `object \| null \| Promise<object \| null>` | `null` | "The initial data with which app loads" |
| `onChange` | `function` | — | Triggered on updates; receives elements, app state, and files |
| `excalidrawAPI` | `function` | — | "Callback triggered with the excalidraw api once rendered" |
| `viewModeEnabled` | `boolean` | — | Controls view-only mode; takes precedence over initial state |
| `theme` | `"light" \| "dark"` | `"light"` | Controls appearance; host app controls unless theme toggle enabled |

## UI & Display

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `UIOptions` | `object` | DEFAULT | Customize canvas actions and UI behavior |
| `zenModeEnabled` | `boolean` | — | Controls zen mode; fully managed by host app |
| `gridModeEnabled` | `boolean` | — | Controls grid display; fully managed by host app |
| `langCode` | `string` | `en` | Language code for UI localization |
| `name` | `string` | — | Drawing name for exports |

## Event Handlers

| Prop | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| `onPointerUpdate` | `function` | Triggered on mouse pointer movement |
| `onPointerDown` | `function` | Triggered on pointer down events |
| `onScrollChange` | `function` | Triggered when canvas scrolls |
| `onPaste` | `function` | Triggered on paste; returns boolean to allow/prevent |
| `onLibraryChange` | `function` | Triggered when library updates |
| `onLinkOpen` | `function` | Triggered when links are clicked |

## Advanced Props

| Prop | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| `generateLinkForSelection` | `function` | Override URL generation for element linking |
| `generateIdForFile` | `function` | Override file ID generation |
| `validateEmbeddable` | `boolean \| string[] \| RegExp \| function` | Custom validator for embeddable URLs |
| `isCollaborating` | `boolean` | Indicates collaboration mode |
| `libraryReturnUrl` | `string` | URL for library installation return |
| `detectScroll` | `boolean` | Enable scroll detection; defaults to `true` |
| `handleKeyboardGlobally` | `boolean` | Bind keyboard to document; defaults to `false` |
| `autoFocus` | `boolean` | Focus component on page load; defaults to `false` |
```
</details></tool-use>

Based on the search results, here's what I found about the Excalidraw export utilities and related APIs:

## Export Functions

The `exportToBlob` function returns a promise which resolves with a blob. It internally uses canvas.ToBlob.

The `exportToSvg` function returns a promise which resolves to svg of the exported drawing. It accepts parameters including `elements` (ExcalidrawElement[]), `appState`, `exportPadding`, `metadata`, and `files`.

## Restore Functions

You can use `restoreAppState` and `restoreElements` to load the app state, and `serializeAsJSON` to save the app state.

The `restoreElements` function will make sure all properties of element is correctly set and if any attribute is missing, it will be set to default value. When localElements are supplied, they are used to ensure that existing restored elements reuse version (and increment it), and regenerate versionNonce.

## Scene Serialization

`serializeAsJSON` takes the scene elements and state and returns a JSON string. Deleted elements as well as most properties from AppState are removed from the resulting JSON.

The serialization process starts with three core data structures (elements, appState, files) and produces format-specific outputs through the serializeAsJSON function, which serves as the central serialization point.

These functions are exported from the `@excalidraw/excalidraw` package and are available for use in React applications.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

```text
# Excalidraw Utility Functions

Based on the documentation provided, here are the available utilities from `@excalidraw/excalidraw`:

## Serialization & Data Loading

**serializeAsJSON**
- Signature: `serializeAsJSON({ elements: ExcalidrawElement[], appState: AppState }): string`
- "Takes the scene elements and state and returns a JSON string"

**serializeLibraryAsJSON**
- Signature: `serializeLibraryAsJSON(libraryItems: LibraryItems[])`
- Converts library items to JSON format

**loadFromBlob**
- Signature: `loadFromBlob(blob: Blob, localAppState: AppState | null, localElements: ExcalidrawElement[] | null, fileHandle?: FileSystemHandle | null) => Promise<RestoredDataState>`
- Loads scene data from a blob/file

**loadLibraryFromBlob**
- Signature: `loadLibraryFromBlob(blob: Blob, defaultStatus: "published" | "unpublished")`
- Loads library data from blob

**loadSceneOrLibraryFromBlob**
- Signature: `loadSceneOrLibraryFromBlob(blob: Blob, localAppState: AppState | null, localElements: ExcalidrawElement[] | null, fileHandle?: FileSystemHandle | null) => Promise<{ type: string, data: RestoredDataState | ImportedLibraryState }>`
- Detects and loads either scene or library from blob

## Element Analysis

**isInvisiblySmallElement**
- Signature: `isInvisiblySmallElement(element: ExcalidrawElement): boolean`
- Returns true if element has zero width/height

**isLinearElement**
- Signature: `isLinearElement(elementType?: ExcalidrawElement): boolean`
- Identifies arrow or line elements

**getNonDeletedElements**
- Signature: `getNonDeletedElements(elements: readonly ExcalidrawElement[]): readonly NonDeletedExcalidrawElement[]`
- Filters out deleted elements

**getFreeDrawSvgPath**
- Signature: `getFreeDrawSvgPath(element: ExcalidrawFreeDrawElement)`
- Returns SVG path for freehand drawings

## Coordinate Conversion

**sceneCoordsToViewportCoords**
- Signature: `sceneCoordsToViewportCoords({ sceneX: number, sceneY: number }, appState: AppState): { x: number, y: number }`
- Converts scene coordinates to viewport coordinates

**viewportCoordsToSceneCoords**
- Signature: `viewportCoordsToSceneCoords({ clientX: number, clientY: number }, appState: AppState): { x: number, y: number }`
- Converts viewport coordinates to scene coordinates

## Bounds & Collision Detection

**getCommonBounds**
- Signature: `getCommonBounds(elements: readonly ExcalidrawElement[]): readonly [minX: number, minY: number, maxX: number, maxY: number]`
- Calculates bounding box for multiple elements

**elementsOverlappingBBox**
- Signature: `elementsOverlappingBBox(elements: readonly NonDeletedExcalidrawElement[], bounds: Bounds | ExcalidrawElement, errorMargin?: number, type: "overlap" | "contain" | "inside"): NonDeletedExcalidrawElement[]`
- Filters elements by spatial relationship with bounds

**isElementInsideBBox**
- Signature: `isElementInsideBBox(element: NonDeletedExcalidrawElement, bounds: Bounds, eitherDirection?: boolean): boolean`
- Tests if single element is inside bounds

**elementPartiallyOverlapsWithOrContainsBBox**
- Signature: `elementPartiallyOverlapsWithOrContainsBBox(element: NonDeletedExcalidrawElement, bounds: Bounds): boolean`
- Checks for overlap or containment with bounds

## Library Management

**mergeLibraryItems**
- Signature: `mergeLibraryItems(localItems: LibraryItems, otherItems: LibraryItems): LibraryItems`
- Merges two library item arrays

**parseLibraryTokensFromUrl**
- Signature: `parseLibraryTokensFromUrl(): { libraryUrl: string; idToken: string | null } | null`
- Extracts library parameters from URL hash

**useHandleLibrary**
- Signature: `useHandleLibrary(opts: { excalidrawAPI: ExcalidrawAPI, getInitialLibraryItems?: () => LibraryItemsSource })`
- React hook for automatic library importing

## Scene & Version Management

**getSceneVersion**
- Signature: `getSceneVersion(elements: ExcalidrawElement[])`
- Returns current scene version

## UI & Localization

**useEditorInterface**
- Returns device info (formFactor, UI mode, touch capabilities, sidebar fit)
- Hook for accessing editor context information

**useI18n**
- Signature: `() => { langCode, t }`
- "A hook that returns the current language code and translation helper function"

**defaultLang**, **languages**
- `defaultLang`: String constant set to `"en"`
- `languages`: Array of supported language codes for the `langCode` prop
```
</details></tool-use>

    10→
    11→export interface TldrawStaticRendererProps {

    13→}
    14→
    15→export const TldrawDisplayRenderer: FC<TldrawStaticRendererProps> = ({
    16→  snapshot,
    17→}) => {
    18→  const theme = useColorScheme()
    19→  const containerRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null)
    20→  const [hasEnteredView, setHasEnteredView] = useState(false)
    21→
    22→  useEffect(() => {
    23→    if (hasEnteredView) return
    24→    const target = containerRef.current
    25→    if (!target) return
    26→
    27→    const observer = new IntersectionObserver(
    28→      (entries) => {
    29→        if (entries.some((e) => e.isIntersecting)) {
    30→          setHasEnteredView(true)
    31→          observer.disconnect()
    32→        }
    33→      },
    34→      { rootMargin: '200px 0px', threshold: 0.1 },
    35→    )
    36→    observer.observe(target)
    37→    return () => observer.disconnect()
    38→  }, [hasEnteredView])
    39→
    40→  if (!hasEnteredView) {
    41→    return (
    42→      <div ref={containerRef} className={css.tldrawStaticContainer}>
    43→        <div className={css.tldrawLoading}>Loading tldraw...</div>
    44→      </div>
    45→    )
    46→  }
    47→
    48→  return (
    49→    <div ref={containerRef}>
    50→      <TldrawStaticCanvas snapshot={snapshot} theme={theme} />
    51→    </div>
    52→  )
    53→}
    54→
    55→// --- Fullscreen readonly content ---
    56→
    57→const TldrawExpandContent: FC<{
    58→  dismiss: () => void
    59→  TldrawComponent: FC<any>
    60→  data: Record<string, any>
    61→  theme: 'light' | 'dark'
    62→}> = ({ dismiss, TldrawComponent, data, theme }) => (

    64→    <div className={css.tldrawDialogHeader}>
    65→      <span className={css.tldrawDialogTitle}>Whiteboard</span>
    66→      <span className={css.tldrawDialogMeta}>tldraw</span>
    67→      <button type="button" className={css.tldrawHeaderClose} onClick={dismiss}>
    68→        <X size={18} />
    69→      </button>
    70→    </div>
    71→    <div className={css.tldrawDialogCanvas}>
    72→      <TldrawComponent
    73→        snapshot={data}
    74→        hideUi
    75→        onMount={(editor: any) => {
    76→          editor.updateInstanceState({ isReadonly: true })
    77→          editor.setCurrentTool('hand')
    78→          editor.user.updateUserPreferences({ colorScheme: theme })
    79→          setTimeout(() => editor.zoomToFit({ animate: false }), 100)
    80→        }}
    81→      />
    82→    </div>
    83→  </>
    84→)
    85→
    86→// --- Main Canvas ---
    87→
    88→const TldrawStaticCanvas: FC<{
    89→  snapshot: string
    90→  theme: 'light' | 'dark'

    99→  const editorRef = useRef<any>(null)
   100→  const { className: portalClassName } = usePortalTheme()
   101→
   102→  useEffect(() => {

   108→      .catch(() => setLibLoading(false))
   109→  }, [])
   110→
   111→  useEffect(() => {
   112→    if (editorRef.current?.user?.updateUserPreferences) {
   113→      editorRef.current.user.updateUserPreferences({ colorScheme: theme })
   114→    }
   115→  }, [theme])
   116→
   117→  const handleMount = useCallback(
   118→    (editor: any) => {
   119→      editorRef.current = editor
   120→      editor.updateInstanceState({ isReadonly: true })
   121→      editor.setCurrentTool('hand')
   122→      editor.user.updateUserPreferences({ colorScheme: theme })
   123→      setTimeout(() => {

   125→      }, 100)
   126→    },
   127→    [theme],
   128→  )
   129→
   130→  const handleExpand = useCallback(() => {
   131→    if (!TldrawComponent || !data) return

   142→      portalClassName,
   143→      theme,
   144→      showCloseButton: false,
   145→      clickOutsideToDismiss: true,
   146→    })
   147→  }, [TldrawComponent, data, theme, portalClassName])
   148→
   149→  const loading = dataLoading || libLoading
   150→
   151→  if (loading) {
   152→    return (
   153→      <div className={css.tldrawStaticContainer}>
   154→        <div className={css.tldrawLoading}>Loading tldraw...</div>
   155→      </div>
   156→    )
   157→  }
   158→
   159→  if (dataError || !data) {

   166→
   167→  if (!TldrawComponent) {

   179→      data-color-scheme={theme}
   180→      suppressHydrationWarning

   193→          className={css.tldrawActionButton}
   194→          title="Zoom In"

   223→      </div>

   226→}
   227→
   228→class TldrawErrorBoundary extends Component<
   229→  { fallback: ReactNode; children: ReactNode },
   230→  { hasError: boolean }
   231→> {
   232→  state = { hasError: false }
   233→  static getDerivedStateFromError() {
   234→    return { hasError: true }
   235→  }
   236→  render() {

   238→  }
   239→}
